name: Sidewalk Chalk
recipeIngredient: 1 cup plaster of Paris (do not pack)
Liquid tempera paint in various colors
Margarine tub or other disposable mixing containers
Disposable molds (e.g., toilet paper rolls with tape covering one end)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the plaster into a container.
2. Using a disposable stick, stir in most of the water until combined.
3. Add 2-3 tablespoons of liquid tempera paint, mixing well, especially at the bottom.
4. Gradually add more water to achieve a thicker consistency and stir thoroughly.
5. Pour the mixture into the molds.
6. Allow the chalk to dry completely before removing from the molds.
This recipe creates chalk that works great on sidewalks but not on chalkboards, as it is too hard. It's ideal for making Christmas gifts or other presents. Warning: This project can be messy.
